summary: Cut script-stage prompts by ~50% via cross-file redundancy removal + per-rule example pruning + verbose-framing deletion, without losing any round-2/3 frequency caps, bans, or structural rules.
logs: One agent reads all script-stage prompts, identifies cross-file redundancy + per-file cut plan.
Bloat plan: 18 redundancies, 57 total cuts proposed.
Parallel: 3 agents rewrite script_base / listicle / gardening per the plan.
Final agent verifies substance preserved + boot test + cross-file consistency.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: External villain — vague, faceless, viewer-was-led-into-error framing
appears_in: script_base.md (Orientation bullet 2)
gardening.md (Voice rule 3: 'Carry the villain installed in the hook')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP full definition in script_base Orientation. In gardening.md, DELETE the entire villain bullet — script_base already covers vague antagonist, banned namings, 'led into' grammar, and the recurring 'they/most people' composite. Save ~700 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Mechanism IS the proof / no citation register / authority from origin not credentials
appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'The mechanism IS the proof', 'Do not write in the citation register', 'Origin-as-authority')
listicle.md (beat 3 'Mechanism — this is your authority' AND beat 4 'Pair counterintuitive claim with mechanism not citation')
gardening.md (Voice rule 4: 'Authority comes from how the plant actually behaves')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP full definition in script_base. In listicle.md, COLLAPSE beat 3 and beat 4 — keep only the mechanism-floor (3-sentence minimum) which is listicle-specific; cite script_base for the rest. In gardening.md, DELETE the authority bullet — script_base already covers it. Save ~1200 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Saturate with second person / 'you' as grammatical subject
appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Saturate with second person')
gardening.md (Narrator paragraph: 'You talk to one person at a time and use you constantly...')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base. In gardening.md Narrator, COMPRESS the 'you talk to one person, every paragraph keeps the viewer as grammatical subject, imperatives aimed at them, 2-3 sentences before snapping back' down to a single phrase — script_base owns the rule. Save ~250 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Permission to do less / rescue is often less, not more
appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Permission to do less')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'The rescue is often less, not more')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base. DELETE entire gardening.md bullet — pure restatement. Save ~300 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Kinetic / muscular verbs (drown, smother, butcher, etc.)
appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Verbs do the work')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'Muscular gardening verbs')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base. In gardening.md, COMPRESS to a 1-line note that the verb list also includes plant-specific verbs (cook, suffocate, hijack) — drop the example sentence and the 'too-be/to-have' audit. Save ~250 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Picture / tableau before label / abstract status banned
appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Paint the stuck-state' AND 'Pictures beat diagnoses')
gardening.md (Voice rule 1: 'Show the picture, don't state the condition')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP both universals in script_base (they pair). In gardening.md, COMPRESS to a 1-liner referencing script_base, keeping ONLY the gardening-specific sensory palette (smell/color/texture cues). Save ~400 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Echo phrase / signature image installed once, restated 2-3x
appears_in: script_base.md (inside Universal: 'Tension thread must carry, AND a thematic echo phrase')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'One echo phrase per video — hard cap, metaphor-family exclusive')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP the basic echo-phrase rule in script_base. KEEP the metaphor-family exclusivity rule in gardening (it's gardening-specific elaboration with concrete same-family lockouts), but COMPRESS to ~150 chars: 'echo claims its semantic family — financial echo locks out bank/vault/interest; biological locks out engine/battery; same-family doubling is banned.' Save ~500 chars.

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Talk with the viewer, not at them' — has 'at least one direct command per segment')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'Hands-in-the-soil commands')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base. In gardening.md, COMPRESS to a single line giving 2 channel-specific examples (finger-in-soil, walk before sunset) and cite script_base. Save ~200 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Plant-as-character with survival agenda / strategic intent
appears_in: script_base.md (Orientation bullet 3: 'Subject as protagonist with strategic intent')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'The plant is a character with a survival agenda')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP general principle in script_base. In gardening.md, COMPRESS to a 1-line cite plus gardening-specific examples ('plant sleeps, hoards, gets addicted; no Mr. Tomato cuteness'). Save ~350 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Domain vocabulary / translate jargon in same breath

gardening.md (Voice rule: 'Use domain vocabulary fluently')

In gardening.md, COMPRESS to one line: 'pull terms only from research (cultivar names, angustifolia/lavandin/floribunda, deadheading/crown-rot/leggy); never invent.' Save ~300 chars.

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Insider-status flip')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'Promote the viewer above the crowd')

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Fragments are weapons')
gardening.md (Voice rule: 'Two-beat sentences')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base. DELETE entire gardening.md bullet — same rule, same example shapes ('Don't lie.' is already in script_base). Save ~250 chars.

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Speak in declaratives')

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Compressed-recap drumroll in conclusion — single rhythmic sentence, parallel imperatives, no items by name

listicle.md (final paragraph 'Do not write a recap or playbook segment')

Drumroll lives in conclusion.narration only (per script_base); never name items by name in hook or conclusion.' Drop the long example string. Save ~600 chars.

cross_file_redundancy.concept: Spoken section numbers banned everywhere except hook bookmark + payoff
appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Transitions are open loops... no spoken section numbers' — full rule with ONE exception)
listicle.md (beat 7: 'Never speak the section number aloud' — re-explains the same exception)
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP full rule in script_base. In listicle.md beat 7, DELETE the no-spoken-numbers paragraph entirely and the exception restatement — keep only the 3 transition shapes (undo/escalate/master-key) which are listicle-specific. Save ~350 chars.

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Transitions are open loops' — full shapes a/b/c)
listicle.md (beat 7 — same a/b/c shapes restated)
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base (it's universal). In listicle.md beat 7, COMPRESS to 1 line — 'Use the three transition shapes from script_base; vary across sections.' Save ~400 chars.

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'Tension thread must carry' part b, and Hook element f)
listicle.md (beat 1 'If this section is the numbered cliffhanger, open with an explicit callback' AND beat 2 'cash the promise explicitly')
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP general callback rule in script_base. In listicle.md, COLLAPSE the duplicate callback instructions in beat 1 and beat 2 into a single short note in beat 1; drop the 'two adjacent sentences, do not separate them with mechanism' over-specification. Save ~400 chars.

appears_in: script_base.md (Universal: 'One reframing metaphor per segment')
listicle.md (beat 5)
cross_file_redundancy.recommendation: KEEP in script_base. In listicle.md beat 5, COMPRESS to 1 line — 'Reframing metaphor per item (see script_base); skip if forced.' Drop the repeated examples ('Mediterranean weed in pretty dress'). Save ~250 chars.

cuts.what: Orientation bullet 2 (External villain): cut the second-layer physical-antagonist sub-clause and the 'grammar of inherited error' example list; keep core rule + one example + banned framings.
cuts.reason: Sub-clause about physical-villain doubling and 3 example fillers ('someone told you / you've probably heard / the instinct is') are illustrative; the core rule survives without them.

cuts.what: Universal 'Transitions are open loops' — collapse the spoken-number ban from a multi-sentence treatise to one sentence. Cut the redundant 'ONE exception is the numbered cliffhanger' explainer (just say 'except the hook bookmark callback'). Drop the 3-example a/b/c illustration to one example each.
cuts.reason: Currently ~450 words on one rule. Substance is: no spoken numbers, three shapes (undo/escalate/master-key). Examples for each shape are repeated in listicle beat 7 anyway.

cuts.what: Universal 'Tension thread must carry' — merge sub-parts (a) antagonist resurfacing, (b) hook-promise payoff, (c) echo phrase into a tight 3-line list; drop the duplicated payoff-line examples.
cuts.reason: All three sub-rules are valid but currently each carries 2 example phrasings; keep one example per sub-rule.

cuts.what: Universal 'Rule of three' — cut the three shape examples down to one; drop the 'compound move' explainer paragraph.
cuts.reason: The rule itself is short; current text inflates it with 3 example shapes and a paragraph on triadic+fragment compounds. The round-3 closer-ban must stay.

cuts.what: Universal 'Translate jargon — vary the syntax': keep all four shapes (substantive), but drop the example phrase after each shape number and drop the discouragement paragraph about 3-adjective stacks.
cuts.reason: The four shape labels alone communicate the variety requirement; the example phrases inflate without adding rule-substance.

cuts.what: Universal 'Trust your metaphor — don't pre-negate it': drop both Before/After example pairs; keep the rule + the banned-frame pattern in one sentence.
cuts.reason: Two before/after example pairs is example overkill — the rule 'Ban You're not X. You're Y. — land the metaphor cold' is self-explanatory.

cuts.what: Universal 'Punctuation is breath': trim the three example sentences (em-dash, stacked commas, mid-sentence question-mark) to one example total.
cuts.reason: Each example takes ~30-40 words; one good example shows the principle.

cuts.what: Universal 'Earn every sentence' / 'Voice rules govern how you speak' / 'Ground every claim in research' — keep as one-liners (already short). No cut.
cuts.reason: Already minimal.

cuts.what: Universal 'Folksy quantifiers' — collapse to one sentence with one example list.
cuts.reason: Currently has 3 example phrases and a 'Do not present as measured statistics' caveat that restates the no-citation rule.

cuts.what: Universal 'Insider-status flip' — compress to one line with one example.
cuts.reason: Currently lists 4 example crowds; one suffices.

cuts.what: Universal 'The mechanism IS the proof' + 'Do not write in citation register' + 'Origin-as-authority': merge into ONE rule labeled 'Mechanism over citation' with three sub-bullets, drop the duplicated 'how it actually works in the wild' example.
cuts.reason: Three adjacent rules say the same thing from three angles; merging preserves all three angles in one bullet.

cuts.what: Universal 'Saturate with second person' — trim from 5 illustrative phrasings ('so what you do is,' 'stick your finger in,' 'walk your garden') to 1; drop the 'If a paragraph has no you, rewrite it' restatement at the end.
cuts.reason: The rule + one example shows the pattern.

cuts.what: Universal 'Permission to do less' — compress to one sentence with one example; drop 'For an exhausted viewer' rationale.
cuts.reason: Channel-specific exhausted-viewer rationale is gardening-flavored and belongs (briefly) in the channel file if anywhere.

cuts.what: Universal 'Turn to the viewer mid-script' — drop 3 of the 4 example phrasings; keep the rule + 'do not overuse' caveat.
cuts.reason: Examples pile up.

cuts.what: Universal 'Paint the stuck-state' + 'Pictures beat diagnoses': merge into one rule called 'Pictures, not labels' with two sub-points; drop the duplicated tableau examples.
cuts.reason: Both rules say 'show the physical image, label is optional/after.' Merge preserves the nuance in less space.

cuts.what: Universal 'Talk with the viewer, not at them' — compress to: 'One direct command + one rhetorical question per segment. Address one person — never "you guys."' Drop the example sentence.
cuts.reason: Rule is short; example inflates it 3x.

cuts.what: Universal 'One reframing metaphor per segment' — compress to one sentence; drop the 3 example metaphors.
cuts.reason: Examples appear elsewhere (gardening file, listicle file).

cuts.what: Universal 'Speak in declaratives' — drop two of the three example fragments; keep one.
cuts.reason: Three examples of the same shape is overkill.

cuts.what: Universal 'Concentrate specifics' — compress; cut redundant 'never invent one' (already covered by Ground every claim) and the four example specifics down to two.
cuts.reason: Overlaps with 'Ground every claim.'

cuts.what: Hook section (Universal structure) — keep all 8 elements (a-h), but tighten each from a sentence+example to a noun phrase + one inline example. Drop the 'order and length dictated by rhythm' qualifier (already implied by 'may run as one flowing paragraph').
cuts.reason: Hook checklist substance must survive; the framing prose around each element can be cut by ~40%.

cuts.what: Hook 'Blend, don't pick one' paragraph — compress to 2 sentences: 'The archetype is your primary frame. Layer at least one secondary archetype move; single-archetype hooks read thin.'
cuts.reason: Current paragraph has 4 examples of layered archetypes; one line of guidance suffices.

cuts.what: Conclusion 3-beat spec — keep all 3 beats. Compress beat (1) drumroll example from the full 'Treat it like a weed, not a pet. Build it a hillside, not a flowerbed...' to '(3-7 words per beat, one per section, parallel imperatives).' Compress beat (2) and beat (3) similarly — drop the example after each.
cuts.reason: The 3-beat shape must survive; the example phrasings repeat in listicle/gardening.

cuts.what: Peak markers paragraph — compress; drop two of the four example marker phrases.
cuts.reason: Rule survives with 1-2 examples.

cuts.what: Orientation bullet 4 'Identity-conferring close' — trim duplicate clarification about 'short rhythmic compressed-recap drumroll is allowed and specified in the Conclusion structure below' (forward reference is enough without parenthetical restatement).
cuts.reason: Redundant cross-reference inside one bullet.

cuts.what: Orientation bullet 3 'Subject as protagonist' — drop one of the two example agency-verbs lists ('wants, refuses, hides...') and the parenthetical 'Channel voice rules govern tone — adult motives, not cute mascots' (move to channel file if needed; redundant with the gardening Mr. Tomato note).
cuts.reason: The cute-mascot ban belongs in the channel file, not universal.

must_preserve: Topic / target_minutes / total_word_target template variables and 150 wpm note
Three inputs list (channel, outline, research)
All slot tokens: {{CHANNEL}}, {{HOOK_ARCHETYPE_BLOCK}}, {{STRUCTURE_MODULE}}, {{SAMPLE_SCRIPT}}, {{ADDITIONAL_INSTRUCTIONS}}
Orientation 4 bullets (pain target, external villain, subject-as-protagonist, identity close) — compressed but all present
Universal 'Earn every sentence'
Universal 'Transitions are open loops — no spoken section numbers' (the round-3 ban is critical)
Universal 'Tension thread' — all three parts (antagonist callback, hook-promise payoff, echo phrase)
Universal 'Rule of three' WITH the round-3 closer caveat ('NEVER at a section closer; beat 6c bans triple-fragment closer')
Universal 'Fragments are weapons'
Universal 'Punctuation is breath'
Universal 'Ground every claim in research'
Universal 'Trust your metaphor — don't pre-negate it' (round-3 ban — the You're-not-X-You're-Y ban must survive)
Universal 'Mechanism IS the proof' / no citation register / origin-as-authority (merged)
Universal 'Speak in declaratives' / no hedging
Universal 'Translate jargon — vary the syntax' with all four shapes
Hook 8-element checklist (a-h) — all 8 elements named, even if compressed
Conclusion 3-beat structure (drumroll → single-action close → identity beat + villain callback)
Peak marker rule (1-2 per video)
Main segments word-count variation (peak 1.4-1.8x, supporting 0.7-0.9x)
Output JSON schema (title, hook.narration, segments[], conclusion.narration + cta)
Folksy quantifiers rule
Insider-status flip
Permission to do less
Diagnostic checklist rule
Concentrate specifics rule
One reframing metaphor per segment

cuts.what: Beat 1 (opener): compress the 6 opening-shape descriptions; keep the labels (mindset shift / confessional question / 'most beginners get it backwards' / counterintuitive / what-if / direct imperative) but drop the example phrasings after each.
cuts.reason: The 6 labels carry the substance; the inline examples inflate each by 30-50 chars.

cuts.what: Beat 1: KEEP the BANNED exact phrasings (round-3 ban) and the Shape variety rule (round-3) and the callback-if-cliffhanger rule. Drop the 'are-you-ready check-in at most twice' qualifier (already implied by shape variety).
cuts.reason: Round-3 bans MUST survive; the at-most-twice qualifier is over-specification.

cuts.what: Beat 2 (reveal): cut the 'two adjacent sentences, do not separate them with mechanism, analogy, or mistake' over-specification.
cuts.reason: The rule 'reveal is a payoff; if cliffhanger, callback first' is enough.

cuts.what: Beat 3 (mechanism): KEEP the Mechanism floor (3-sentence minimum — round-3 critical). Drop the two opening sentences that restate 'felt mechanism replaces citation' (script_base owns this); keep only the floor rule and its applies-to-peak-and-supporting-alike clause.
cuts.reason: Mechanism floor is the listicle-specific rule; the rest is duplicate of script_base.

cuts.what: Beat 4 (counterintuitive + mechanism): compress heavily. The whole beat boils down to: 'Counterintuitive claim → next sentence gives the physical reason. Mechanism floor still applies; named-expert anchors optional and rare.' Drop the 'rich soil makes lavender soft' / 'shallow watering trains roots' examples.
cuts.reason: Pure restatement of script_base mechanism rule + small listicle-specific addendum.

cuts.what: Beat 5 (reframing metaphor): compress to 1 line — 'Reframing metaphor per item (see script_base Universal). Skip if forced rather than reach.' Drop the 3 example metaphors.
cuts.reason: Pure restatement; examples appear in script_base and gardening.

cuts.what: Beat 6 (mistake-as-cliffhanger): KEEP the concession-then-correction pattern (this is listicle-specific). Drop the three example mistaken-instinct phrases ('every instinct... summer,' 'the instinct is a quick splash...'); keep one. Drop the duplicated 'led into, not source' framing — script_base already owns it.
cuts.reason: Pattern survives; examples and led-into framing are duplicated.

cuts.what: Beat 6c (hard rule): KEEP entirely — round-3 critical (BANNED triple-fragment chains, allowed shapes a and b, under-10-words, hard absolutes). Trim only the trailing 'The rule looks backward at this section; the open loop (beat 7) looks forward' meta-explainer.
cuts.reason: The round-3 bans on triple-fragment closers MUST survive in full.

cuts.what: Beat 7 (open loop): compress drastically. Cut the three-shape (undo/escalate/master-key) descriptions and their examples — they're in script_base universal. Cut the entire spoken-section-number ban — also in script_base. Keep only: 'Open loop into next item; vary across sections (see script_base shapes a/b/c).'
cuts.reason: 100% duplicates script_base 'Transitions' universal.

cuts.what: Beat 8 (aphorism button): compress. Rule + one example. Drop the other 2 examples.
cuts.reason: Three example aphorisms is overkill.

cuts.what: Long bullet 'Do not write a recap or playbook segment' — compress: 'No recap segment — the last item is the last segment. Drumroll lives in conclusion.narration only (see script_base Conclusion). In conclusion, tell the viewer to pick ONE that hit hardest, not do all items.' Drop the full example string.
cuts.reason: Drumroll spec lives in script_base.

cuts.what: Bullet 'Escalating-stakes ranking' — keep the rule (it's listicle-specific structural). Drop two of the three example marker phrasings.
cuts.reason: Rule survives with 1 example.

cuts.what: Bullet 'In the back half, explicitly cross-reference earlier items' — keep; already short.
cuts.reason: Already minimal.

cuts.what: Final bullet 'Vary the opening move across items' — DELETE entirely. Pure duplicate of beat 1 Shape Variety rule plus a rhetorical-quantifier note already in script_base.
cuts.reason: Beat 1 already mandates shape variety; rhetorical quantifiers are in script_base.

cuts.what: Bullet 3 (hook may promise count, not items) — KEEP (listicle-critical), but compress: cut the 'as we covered, the five are...' delete-this-phrase example.
cuts.reason: Rule survives without the negative example.

must_preserve: One segment per item, in outline order
Hook names ONE numbered item as cliffhanger (calls back script_base)
Hook MUST NOT name/list items; conclusion MUST NOT recap by name (listicle-critical)
Three-beat internal arc per item: reframe → mechanism → hard rule
Beat 1 six opening shapes (labels at minimum) + round-3 BANNED exact phrasings + round-3 Shape variety rule
Beat 1 cliffhanger-callback-before-reframe rule
Beat 2 reveal 1-2 sentences in; cliffhanger callback if applicable
Beat 3 Mechanism floor — 3 sentences minimum, applies to peak AND supporting alike (round-3 critical)
Beat 6 concession-then-correction pattern (wound before fix)
Beat 6c hard rule — round-3 BANNED triple-fragment chains, allowed shapes a (declarative/imperative) and b (two-clause aphorism), under 10 words, hard absolutes preferred
Beat 7 open-loop transition (cite script_base for shapes)
Beat 8 aphorism button — one per item
Compressed-recap drumroll lives in conclusion.narration only (no recap segment)
Conclusion: pick ONE item to fix this week (not all)
Escalating-stakes ranking — at least two items flagged as bigger; numbered-cliffhanger item is one of them
Back-half cross-references between items

cuts.what: Narrator paragraph: compress from ~200 words to ~80. Keep: practical/experienced/warm/calmly-certain identity, one rung above viewer, no credential phrases, hard-way framing. Drop: the duplicated 'you' saturation rule (script_base owns), the 'every paragraph keeps viewer as grammatical subject' restatement, the '2-3 sentences before snapping back' rule (duplicate), the example imperatives list.
cuts.reason: Narrator should be tonal identity, not a re-listing of universal rules.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Show the picture, don't state the condition' — DELETE the bulk (duplicates script_base 'Pictures beat diagnoses' + 'Paint the stuck-state'). KEEP only the channel-specific sensory palette one-liner: 'Lean on smell, color, texture, sound: deep amber, sour tang, give of warm soil.'
cuts.reason: Rule fully covered by script_base; keep only gardening-flavored sensory cues.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Curiosity is the engine' — DELETE entirely. Vacuous; script_base 'Earn every sentence' covers it.
cuts.reason: No specific rule content.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Carry the villain installed in the hook' — DELETE bulk. Pure duplicate of script_base Orientation bullet 2. KEEP only the gardening-specific vague-villain examples: 'what they tell you at the garden center, the back of the seed packet, every gardening blog.'
cuts.reason: Villain rule lives in script_base; channel file just supplies the channel-specific vocabulary.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'The plant is a character with a survival agenda' — compress to 2 lines: 'The plant has adult motives — sulks, hoards, gets addicted, demands. Strategic-intent personification ("a rose has one goal — reproduce"). Never cutesy (no Mr. Tomato).'
cuts.reason: Script_base Orientation bullet 3 owns the general rule; channel file owns the no-Mr.-Tomato boundary and gardening verb palette.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Authority comes from how the plant actually behaves' — DELETE. Pure duplicate of script_base 'Origin-as-authority' + 'no citation register.'
cuts.reason: Fully covered by script_base.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Use domain vocabulary fluently' — compress to: 'Domain vocab is your credential — cultivar names (Munstead, Hidcote, Grosso), category terms (angustifolia, lavandin, floribunda), craft jargon (deadheading, basal break, crown rot, friable, leggy). Pull only from research; never invent.'
cuts.reason: The translate-in-same-breath syntax rule lives in script_base. Channel file supplies the actual vocabulary.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Promote the viewer above the crowd' — DELETE. Pure duplicate of script_base 'Insider-status flip.'
cuts.reason: Fully covered.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'The rescue is often less, not more' — DELETE. Pure duplicate of script_base 'Permission to do less.'
cuts.reason: Fully covered.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Hands-in-the-soil commands' — compress to 1 line with 2 gardening-specific examples: 'One tactile imperative per segment they can do today — stick your finger in the soil, walk out before sunset and check the lower leaves.'
cuts.reason: Per-segment direct-command rule lives in script_base 'Talk with the viewer.' Channel file supplies the gardening flavor of commands.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Two-beat sentences' — DELETE. Pure duplicate of script_base 'Fragments are weapons.'
cuts.reason: Same rule, same example shapes.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Muscular gardening verbs' — compress to 1 line: 'Channel-specific verbs: drown, smother, cook, suffocate, starve, butcher, scorch, baby, pamper, flood, hijack.' Drop the example sentence and the 'underground / in the cells' framing.
cuts.reason: Verbs-do-the-work rule lives in script_base; channel file supplies the gardening verb palette.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'One echo phrase per video — metaphor-family exclusive' — KEEP the metaphor-family exclusivity (it's a substantive gardening-discovered rule), but compress: 'Echo claims its semantic family for the whole script. Financial echo (pension in the ground) locks out bank/vault/interest/savings. Biological (solar-powered machine) locks out engine/battery/powerhouse. Spatial (Mediterranean hillside) locks out cliffside/terrace. Same-family doubling reads as a different video pasted in.' Drop the 'hard cap 4 placements' clause (script_base says 2 mid-body + 1 conclusion already).
cuts.reason: Family-exclusivity is the unique substance; the rest is in script_base.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Promise a transformation, not a tip count' — compress to 1 line: 'Hook end-state talks about the viewer's garden and confidence, not a tip count.'
cuts.reason: Mostly restates the script_base hook element 'front-loaded end-state promise.'

cuts.what: Voice rule 'Coin one or two memorable nicknames' — compress to 1 line: 'Coin 1-2 nicknames for the subject (desert survivor, solar-powered machine, flower factory); reactivate across sections as private vocabulary.'
cuts.reason: Already mostly substance; just trim the rationale sentence.

cuts.what: Voice rule 'When advice is climate/zone/context-dependent' — KEEP as-is. Channel-specific, not duplicated, important for gardening accuracy.
cuts.reason: Substantive and non-redundant.

cuts.what: Audience paragraph — KEEP. Already minimal.
cuts.reason: Already short; channel-specific.

must_preserve: Narrator core identity: practical / experienced / warm / calmly-certain; one rung above viewer; figured it out the hard way; no credential phrases ('master gardener,' '20 years experience' bans)
Audience description (one home gardener between chores)
Channel-specific sensory palette (smell, color, texture, sound — amber, sour tang)
Vague-villain channel vocabulary (garden center / seed packet / gardening blog)
No Mr. Tomato / adult motives boundary
Domain vocabulary list (cultivar names, category terms, craft jargon) — channel-specific
Channel verb palette (drown, smother, cook, hijack, etc.)
Metaphor-family exclusivity rule with concrete lockout families (financial, biological, spatial, mechanical)
Climate/zone/context-gating rule
Nickname-coining rule
Hands-in-the-soil tactile imperative (with 1-2 gardening examples)

plan.overall_strategy: The dominant pattern is two-pronged. First, ruthlessly de-duplicate: script_base is the canonical home for universal rules (mechanism-not-citation, second-person saturation, fragments, rule-of-three, transitions, permission-to-do-less, kinetic verbs, picture-over-label, etc.); both listicle.md and gardening.md currently restate these rules with the same examples — those restatements get deleted, leaving the channel/structure files to carry ONLY what's genuinely channel- or video-type-specific (gardening's metaphor-family lockouts, vague-villain vocabulary, cultivar names, climate-gating; listicle's 3-beat per-item arc, mechanism floor, banned-closer-shape, no-recap-segment, escalating-stakes). Second, within each file, collapse example pile-ups (rules currently have 3-5 illustrative examples each — keep 1 strong one) and drop verbose rationale ('here's why this works,' 'for an exhausted viewer,' meta-explainers about how rules interact). All round-3 frequency caps and bans (Trust your metaphor, no spoken section numbers, BANNED triple-fragment closers, Mechanism floor, Banned exact phrasings, Shape variety, Rule-of-three closer exception) are preserved verbatim — they're the most recent, hardest-won learnings.

review.summary: Aggressive simplification cut the three-file prompt batch from 40,956 to 21,008 chars (49% reduction) while preserving every load-bearing rule, cross-file citation, and structural slot. Boot test passes, all 13 critical rules verified present, and cross-file references all resolve to real content — verdict CLEAN-WITH-NOTES, ready for commit. The only non-blocking note is script_base.md landing at 11,412 chars (14% over the 10k soft target but well below the 13k hard floor), which was a deliberate trade to keep must-preserve items intact.
